title: Supervised learning workflow
content:
## Linear regression workflow.
**Collect data** 
Collecting data involves gathering relevant CSV's, databases, Excel, APIs etc
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/6cs1ba4mlp6t7lj94hrw.png)
**Prepare data**
Data pre-processing involves cleaning, handling missing values, removing duplicates etc
###examples.
`df.info()` - gets a summary of the dataset structure in pandas.
`df.drop(columns = ['Cabin'], inplace = True)` - dropping columns with lots of missing values.
`df['Age'] = df['Age'].fillna(df['Age'].median())
df.info()` - filling missing values with median. Used median in case of outliers.
**Visualization**.
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/dadmgperxdu29opbz2do.png)
###example heatmap.
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/s9haoc41ok4cddx2hfq7.png)
**Split data into train and test sets**.
Splitting data helps avoid overfitting of the models i.e. models performing very well on training data but poorly on new data. It also ensures the model doesn't memorize data. 
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/mf0jqm92fcmfp611bpk2.png)
**Training the model**
 Training involves adjusting model parameters to minimize error using training data.
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/97rirpttflaavzcdocwb.png)
**Make predictions on the test data**.
After training, the model uses learned patterns to predict outcomes on new data.
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/5in85l7txans51sak8rp.png)
**Evaluate the model's performance**.
Model evaluation involves measuring how well predictions match real values using test data
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/3nxvzcvmxirtd9yzl4af.png)
## Regularization.
Regularization is a technique used to reduce overfitting by preventing the model from becoming too complex.

##Ridge (l2) and Lasso (L1)
### Ridge
Adds squared penalty on coefficients. Shrinks coefficients but doesn’t make them zero. Keeps all features but reduces magnitude of weights
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/77qyv0q9b0f6rwb1maxc.png)
### Lasso
Adds absolute value penalty. Can reduce some coefficients to zero. it removes useless features. 
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/4ibh0n1r172tz5oiza4p.png)

# Classification.
## Logistic regression workflow.
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/m5ko1duulbgmbw2a6cdp.png)
**split data**
Splitting data helps avoid overfitting of the models i.e. models performing very well on training data but poorly on new data. It also ensures the model doesn't memorize data.
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/e0wnjvtxddxv3fynvtas.png)
**scaling data**
Feature scaling is the process of putting all numerical features on a similar scale so that no feature dominates others just because of its size.
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/jtuqfj7kb795hvyd26k5.png)
**Training the model.**
 Training involves adjusting model parameters to minimize error using training data.
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/0avq69s0x64avwpfqhrr.png)
**making predictions**
After training, the model uses learned patterns to predict outcomes on new data.
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/vhm69s3ele8i4vxunc2l.png)
**Evaluating the model**
Model evaluation involves measuring how well predictions match real values using test data
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/v9co3fj5dgbq3csw2fja.png)
**Display the confusion matrix**
The confusion matrix is used to evaluate classification models by showing correct and incorrect predictions.
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/5rd14uq4z3y39a4gymx6.png)
###Example of confusion matrix.
![Image description](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/cwsyj2gg79jpgfq0kqh4.png)
